works.name: General / Key Works
works.description: Analysis from 1 tossups and 11 bonuses. See clues file for detailed breakdown.

clues.clue: The strangest artist in this movement developed a weird "inhalation" theory of art and created an austere landscape with three unicorns at the bottom.
examples: The strangest artist in this movement developed a weird "inhalation" theory of art and created an austere landscape with three unicorns at the bottom.

clues.clue: Inspired by Manet's Bar at the Folies-Bergere, an artist from this movement painted his wife in a mirror behind the proprietor of a cafe in Chez Mouquin.
examples: Inspired by Manet's Bar at the Folies-Bergere, an artist from this movement painted his wife in a mirror behind the proprietor of a cafe in Chez Mouquin.

clues.clue: The Macbeth Galleries housed the biggest exhibition of artists in this movement, many of whom had met while studying under Thomas Anshutz.
examples: The Macbeth Galleries housed the biggest exhibition of artists in this movement, many of whom had met while studying under Thomas Anshutz.

clues.clue: (*) Arthur Davies later joined this movement, whose original members included John Sloan and William Glackens.
examples: (*) Arthur Davies later joined this movement, whose original members included John Sloan and William Glackens.

clues.clue: Its ringleader painted a carriage on a snowy street, though equally famous today is an artist from this movement known for his boxing scenes.
examples: Its ringleader painted a carriage on a snowy street, though equally famous today is an artist from this movement known for his boxing scenes.
